name: Filled Apricot Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Fresh apples; peeled and sliced
recipeInstructions:
Bars: In a medium bowl, mix the white and whole wheat flours and baking soda. Mix well and set aside. In a large bowl using an electric mixer, combine butter and sugar at medium speed. Add honey, egg, and vanilla and beat until smooth. Add the flour mixture into the honey mixture and blend at low speed just until combined. Do not overmix. Gather dough into a ball, divide in half, roll each into cylinders, wrap each in plastic wrap, and chill for 1 hour.
Filling: In a saucepan, mix together apricots, apples, brown sugar, and water over medium-low heat. Stir until the sugar dissolves. Turn the heat to medium and simmer. Stir until the mixture thickens and most of the liquid evaporates. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature. Puree the filling in a food processor.
To assemble: Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C). Roll each dough cylinder out to about 12 inches long. Place one at a time on a floured board and roll each out to a rectangle about 5 inches wide, 18 inches long, and about 1/8 inch thick. Spread half of the filling down the center of each rectangle and fold each side lengthwise over the filling, overlapping by about 1/2 inch. Cut each into halves for easier handling. Transfer to an ungreased baking sheet, turning over so the seam side is down. Repeat with the other pieces.
Bake for 25 minutes or until the bars begin to turn light golden in color and do not brown. Cool on the sheet for about 2 minutes, then transfer to a cool surface. When the bars reach room temperature, cut each into 8 pieces with a thin, sharp knife.
